other_observers: None, although I left phone messages for other Durango birders.; I am the original observer and no one has acknowledged seeing the bird as of 5:00 PM.
description: Large plegadis ibis with dark brown plumage with irridescant highlights of green. Brownish-gray bill was long and decurved. The facial skin was not red or reddish but rather grayish with bluish-white edges. These edges did not extend behind the eye. The upper edge reached the midpoint of the eye and the lower edge stopped just short of the midpoint of the eye. The surrounding area of the face lacked any white feathering. The eye was completely dark brown without any red or reddish highlight present. The legs were grayish-brown and the knee joint seemed very slightly reddish in tone.Behaviors: The bird was associating with a loose flock of about 75 White-faced Ibises all of which had reddish facial skin and varing amounts of white feathering around the facial skin. It was feeding in shallow water near the water's edge. There was nothing striking about this bird's behavior except it remained near the edge of the flock while I observed. It moved with the flock.Call: Not heardPlumage: Alternate
